author: śrīla bhaktirakṣaka śrīdhara gosvāmī mahārāja
verses:
sujanārbuda⋅rādhita⋅pāda⋅yugaṁ
jugadharma⋅dhurandhara⋅pātra⋅varam
varadābhaya⋅dāyaka⋅pūjya⋅padaṁ
praṇamāmi sadā prabhupāda⋅padam

bhajanorjita⋅sajjana⋅saṅgha⋅patiṁ
patitādhika⋅kāruṇikaikagatim
gativañcita⋅vañcakācintya⋅padaṁ
praṇamāmi sadā prabhupāda⋅padam

atikomala⋅kāñcana⋅dīrghatanuṁ
tanunindita⋅hema⋅mṛṇālamadam
madanārbuda⋅vandita⋅candra⋅padaṁ
praṇamāmi sadā prabhupāda⋅padam

nijasevaka⋅tāraka⋅rañjitavidhuṁ
vidhutāhita⋅huṅkṛta⋅siŋha⋅varam
varaṇāgata⋅bāliśa⋅śanda⋅padaṁ
praṇamāmi sadā prabhupāda⋅padam

vipulīkṛta⋅vaibhava⋅gaura⋅bhuvaṁ
bhuvaneṣu vikīrtita⋅gaura⋅dayam
dayanīyagaṇārpita⋅gaura⋅padaṁ
praṇamāmi sadā prabhupāda⋅padam

ciragaura⋅janāśraya⋅viśvaguruṁ
guru⋅gaura⋅kiśoraka⋅dāsya⋅param
paramādṛta⋅bhaktivinoda⋅padaṁ
praṇamāmi sadā prabhupāda⋅padam

raghu⋅rūpa⋅sanātana⋅kīrtidharaṁ
dharaṇītala⋅kīrtita⋅jīva⋅kavim
kavirāja⋅narottama⋅sakhya⋅padaṁ
praṇamāmi sadā prabhupāda⋅padam

kṛpayā⋅harikīrtana⋅mūrti⋅dharaṁ
dharaṇī⋅bharahāraka⋅gaura⋅janam
janakādhika⋅vatsala⋅snigdha⋅padaṁ
praṇamāmi sadā prabhupāda⋅padam

śaraṇāgata⋅kiṅkara⋅kalpataruṁ
tarudhikkṛta⋅dhīra⋅vadānya⋅varam
varadendra⋅gaṇārcita⋅divya⋅padaṁ
praṇamāmi sadā prabhupāda⋅padam

parahaṁsavaraṁ paramārtha⋅patiṁ
patitoddharaṇe kṛta⋅veśayatim
jatirājagaṇaiḥ parisevya⋅padaṁ
praṇamāmi sadā prabhupāda⋅padam

vṛṣabhānusutā⋅dayitānucaraṁ
caraṇāśrita⋅reṇudharastamaham
mahadadbhuta⋅pāvana⋅śakti⋅padaṁ
praṇamāmi sadā prabhupāda⋅padam
en_translation:
I always offer my respectful obeisances to the lotus feet of Prabhupāda, which are worshiped by millions of good people—the best of personalities who uphold the religious principles for this age, and whose worshipable feet grant boons and fearlessness.

I always offer my respectful obeisances to the lotus feet of Prabhupāda, the leader of the assembly of pure devotees enriched by worship, the sole shelter for those who are most fallen and compassionate, and whose inconceivable lotus feet are the destination for those deprived of true progress and even for those who have been deceived.

I always offer my respectful obeisances to the lotus feet of Prabhupāda, whose long, extremely tender body is like molten gold, who defies the pride of golden lotus stems with his complexion, and whose moon-like feet are adored by millions of Cupids.

I always offer my respectful obeisances to the lotus feet of Prabhupāda, who is like a moon delighting his own stars of servants, who is like a supreme lion roaring against the inimical, and whose lotus feet are a shelter for the foolish who seek refuge.

I always offer my respectful obeisances to the lotus feet of Prabhupāda, who has extensively glorified the land of Lord Gaura, whose mercy is widely distributed throughout the universes, and whose lotus feet are offered to the pitiable souls by the Lord Himself.

I always offer my respectful obeisances to the lotus feet of Prabhupāda, who is the world-teacher and the eternal shelter of the lineage of Lord Gaura, who is devoted to the service of his own spiritual master, Gaura Kiśora, and whose lotus feet are supremely revered by Bhaktivinoda.

I always offer my respectful obeisances to the lotus feet of Prabhupāda, who upholds the glories of the Gosvāmīs—Raghunātha, Rūpa, and Sanātana—the poet of the souls whose glories are sung on earth, and whose lotus feet are befriended by Kavirāja and Narottama.

I always offer my respectful obeisances to the lotus feet of Prabhupāda, who, out of mercy, has embodied the chanting of the glories of God, who removes the burden of the earth, and whose lotus feet are more affectionate and loving than those of a father.

I always offer my respectful obeisances to the lotus feet of Prabhupāda, who is a wish-fulfilling tree for surrendered servants, who is superior to any ordinary tree—being steadfast, benevolent, and the best—and whose divine lotus feet are worshiped by the best of boon-givers.

I always offer my respectful obeisances to the lotus feet of Prabhupāda, who is the best among the highest renunciants and the master of the ultimate truth, who has taken the vow of an ascetic for the upliftment of the fallen, and whose lotus feet are served by the kings of ascetics.

I always offer my respectful obeisances to the lotus feet of Prabhupāda, who is the dear follower of the daughter of Vṛṣabhānu. I am merely a speck of dust at his lotus feet, which possess a greatly wonderful purifying power.
